}\n228\t\n229\t  const upperQuery = originalQuery.toUpperCase();\n230\t  if (station.lines.some((line) => line === upperQuery)) {\n231\t    return true;\n232\t  }\n233\t\n234\t  const expandedName = }\n238\t\n239\t  return false;\n240\t}\n241\t\n242\tfunction scoreSearchResult(station: Station, query: string): number {\n243\t  const normalizedQuery = normalizeForSearch(query);\n244\t  const Supports brotli, gzip, and deflate based on Accept-Encoding header.\n290\t * Brotli is preferred for best compression ratio.\n291\t */\n292\tfunction compressionMiddleware(): MiddlewareHandler {\n293\t  return async (c, next) => {\n294\t    await next();\n295\t\n296\t    const body = c.res.body;\n297\t    if (!body || c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Encoding\")) return;\n298\t\n299\t    const contentType = c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Type\");\n300\t    if (!contentType?.includes(\"json\") && !contentType?.includes(\"text\")) return;\n301\t\n302\t    const acceptEncoding = c.req.header(\"Accept-Encoding\") || \"\";\n303\t    const contentLength = c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Length\");\n304\t\n305\t    // Skip small responses (not worth compressing)\n306\t    if (contentLength && parseInt(contentLength, 10) < 500) return;\n307\t\n308\t    // Clone response to get the body as buffer\n309\t    const buffer = Buffer.from(await c.res.clone().arrayBuffer());\n310\t\n311\t    // Prefer brotli for best compression, then gzip, then deflate\n312\t    if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"br\")) {\n313\t      const brotlied = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n314\t        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n315\t        const brotli = createBrotliCompress();\n316\t        brotli.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n317\t        brotli.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n318\t        brotli.on(\"error\", reject);\n319\t        brotli.end(buffer);\n320\t      });\n321\t\n322\t      c.res = new Response(brotlied, {\n323\t        status: c.res.status,\n324\t        headers: c.res.headers,\n325\t      });\n326\t      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"br\");\n327\t      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n328\t    } else if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"gzip\")) {\n329\t      const gzipped = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n330\t        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n331\t        const gzip = createGzip();\n332\t        gzip.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n333\t        gzip.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n334\t        gzip.on(\"error\", reject);\n335\t        gzip.end(buffer);\n336\t      });\n337\t\n338\t      c.res = new Response(gzipped, {\n339\t        status: c.res.status,\n340\t        headers: c.res.headers,\n341\t      });\n342\t      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"gzip\");\n343\t      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n344\t    } createDeflate();\n348\t        deflate.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n349\t        deflate.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n350\t        deflate.on(\"error\", });\n358\t      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"deflate\");\n359\t      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n360\t    }\n361\t  };\n362\t}\n363\t\n364\t/**\n365\t * Check if a path has \"/api/csrf-token\",\n506\t        \"/api/security/csp-report\", // CSP violation reports (browser-initiated)\n507\t      ],\n508\t    })\n509\t  );\n510\t\n511\t  // HPP protection for all API routes (prevents parameter pollution attacks)\n512\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", hppProtection({ strategy: \"first\" }));\n513\t\n514\t  // HTTP metrics collection for all API routes\n515\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", httpMetrics());\n516\t\n517\t  // Rate limiting on all API routes (60 req/min per IP, token bucket)\n518\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", rateLimiter());\n519\t\n520\t  // Response size limits for API routes (OWASP A01, A04)\n521\t  // Prevents DoS via large response payloads\n522\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", responseSizeLimits());\n523\t\n524\t  // Compression for API responses\n525\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", compressionMiddleware());\n526\t\n527\t  const stationToComplex = buildStationToComplexMap(complexes);\n528\t\n529\t  // Create transfer engine for commute analysis\n530\t  const transferEngine = createTransferEngine({\n531\t    stations,\n532\t    routes,\n533\t    transfers,\n534\t    complexes,\n535\t    getArrivals: (stationId: string) => {\n536\t      return getArrivals(stationId);\n537\t    },\n538\t  });\n539\t\n540\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n541\t  // CSRF token endpoint\n542\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n543\t  app.get(\n544\t    \"/api/csrf-token\",\n545\t    cors({\n546\t      allowedOrigins: [\"*\"], // Allow all origins for CSRF token\n547\t      allowedMethods: [\"GET\"],\n548\t      allowedHeaders: [\"Content-Type\", \"Authorization\"],\n549\t    }),\n550\t    (c) => {\n551\t      const token = generateCsrfToken();\n552\t\n553\t      return c.json({ token });\n554\t    }\n555\t  );\n556\t\n557\t  /**\n558\t   * Format a timestamp as \"X ago\" for human-readable display\n559\t   */\n560\t  function timeAgo(isoString: string | null): string {\n561\t    if (!isoString) return \"Never\";\n562\t    const ms = Date.now() - new Date(isoString).getTime();\n563\t    const seconds = Math.floor(ms / 1000);\n564\t    if (seconds < 60) return `${seconds}s ago`;\n565\t    const minutes = Math.floor(seconds / 60);\n566\t    if (minutes < 60) return `${minutes}m ago`;\n567\t    const hours = Math.floor(minutes / 60);\n568\t    if (hours < 24) return `${hours}h ago`;\n569\t    const days = Math.floor(hours / 24);\n570\t    return `${days}d ago`;\n571\t  }\n572\t\n573\t  /**\n574\t   * Format bytes as human-readable (e.g., \"45.2 MB\")\n575\t   */\n576\t  function formatBytes(bytes: number): string {\n577\t    const units = [\"B\", \"KB\", \"MB\", \"GB\"];\n578\t    let i = 0;\n579\t    while (bytes >= 1024 && i < units.length - 1) {\n580\t      bytes /= 1024;\n581\t      i++;\n582\t    }\n583\t    return `${bytes.toFixed(1)} ${units[i]}`;\n584\t  }\n585\t\n586\t solid ${statusColor}40;\n682\t    }\n683\t    .status-icon { font-size: 20px; }\n684\t    .status-text { color: ${statusColor}; }\n685\t    .grid { display: grid; gap: 20px; }\n686\t    .card {\n687\t          </tr>\n829\t          `\n830\t              )\n831\t              .join(\"\")}\n832\t          </tbody>\n833\t        </table>\n834\t      </div>\n835\t\n836\t      <!-- Alerts Status    </div>\n891\t        </div>\n892\t      </div>\n893\t    </div>\n894\t\n895\t    <div class=\"last-updated\">\n896\t      Last updated: ${new Date(healthData.timestamp).toLocaleString()} •\n897\t      <a href=\"/api/health\" style=\"color: #38bdf8;\">View JSON</a>\n898\t    </div>\n899\t  </div>\n900\t</body>\n901\t</html>`;\n902\t  }\n903\t\n904\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n905\t  // Health endpoint\n906\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n907\t  /**\n908\t   * GET /api/health — Per-feed health status (JSON)\n909\t   *\n910\t   * Query validation: Uses `emptyQuerySchema` (`z.object({}).strict()`).\n911\t   * Any query parameters at all cause a 400 with `{ error: \"validation failed\", details: [...] }`.\n912\t   * No query parameters are accepted.\n913\t   *\n914\t   * Response top-level fields:\n915\t   *   status              (\"ok\" | \"degraded\")\n916\t   *     — \"ok\" when all feeds have circuitOpenAt===null AND lastSuccessAt!==null AND !isStale,\n917\t   *       AND alerts has circuitOpen===false AND lastSuccessAt!==null.\n918\t   *     — \"degraded\" otherwise.\n919\t   *\n920\t   *   timestamp           string (ISO 8601) — new Date().toISOString() at request time.\n921\t   *\n922\t   *   uptime_seconds      number — seconds since SERVER_START_MS (set at module load).\n923\t   *\n924\t   *   feeds               Array<FeedState> — one entry per GTFS-realtime feed.\n925\t   *     Each element:\n926\t   *       id                    string   — feed identifier (e.g. \"1\", \"26\", \"31\", \"36\").\n927\t   *       name                  string   — human-readable feed name.\n928\t   *       status                (\"circuit_open\" | \"never_polled\" | \"stale\" | \"ok\")\n929\t   *         — \"circuit_open\" when circuitOpenAt !== null.\n930\t   *         — \"never_polled\" when lastSuccessAt === null.\n931\t   *         — \"stale\" when isStale === true.\n932\t   *         — \"ok\" otherwise.\n933\t   *       lastSuccessAt         string | null — ISO 8601 of last successful poll, or null.\n934\t   *       lastPollAt            string | null — ISO 8601 of last poll attempt, or null.\n935\t   *       consecutiveFailures  number — count of consecutive failed polls.\n936\t   *       entityCount           number — number of entities in the feed.\n937\t   *       lastError             string | null — truncated (max 100 chars) last error message,\n938\t   *                                  newlines stripped; null if no error.\n939\t   *       tripReplacementPeriod string | null — the feed's trip replacement period.\n940\t   *       avgLatencyMs          number — average of latencyHistory entries.\n941\t   *       errorCount24h         number — count of error timestamps within the last 24 hours.\n942\t   *       parseErrors           (feed-specific type) — parse error details for the feed.\n943\t   *\n944\t   *   alerts              object — alerts polling subsystem status.\n945\t   *     count                  number  — number of alerts tracked.\n946\t   *     lastSuccessAt          number | null — epoch-ms of last successful alert fetch, or null.\n947\t   *     matchRate              number  — fraction of alerts that matched trips (0–1).\n948\t   *     consecutiveFailures    number  — consecutive alert fetch failures.\n949\t   *     circuitOpen            boolean — whether the alerts circuit breaker is open.\n950\t   *     unmatchedCount         number  — alerts that did not match any known trip.\n951\t   *\n952\t   *   delayDetector       object — delay detector subsystem status.\n953\t   *     trackedTrips           number — count of trips currently being tracked.\n954\t   *     activeAlerts           number — count of active predicted delay alerts.\n955\t   *     thresholdMultiplier    number — delay threshold configuration multiplier.\n956\t   *     minTrainsForLineAlert  number — minimum trains needed to trigger a line-level alert.\n957\t   *\n958\t   *   delayPredictor     object — delay predictor subsystem status.\n959\t   *     totalRecords           number — count of delay records stored.\n960\t   *     aggregatedPatterns     number — count of aggregated statistical patterns.\n961\t   *     minObservations        number — minimum observations required for a prediction.\n962\t   *     currentWeather         WeatherCondition — current weather string (e.g. \"clear\").\n963\t   *\n964\t   *   equipment           object (EquipmentCacheStatus) — elevator/escalator outage status.\n965\t   *     lastFetchAt            string | null — ISO 8601 of last fetch attempt.\n966\t   *     lastSuccessAt          string | null — ISO 8601 of last successful fetch.\n967\t   *     outageCount            number — number of stations with tracked outages.\n968\t   *     consecutiveFailures    number — consecutive equipment fetch failures.\n969\t   *     circuitOpen            boolean — whether the equipment circuit breaker is open.\n970\t   *\n971\t   *   pushSubscriptions   number — count of active push notification subscriptions.\n972\t   *\n973\t   *   cacheHitRate        number — fraction 0–1, computed as cache_hits_total /\n974\t   *                            (cache_hits_total + cache_misses_total) summed across all\n975\t   *                            label combinations. Rounded to 2 decimal places.\n976\t   *                            Returns 0 when there have been no cache requests.\n977\t   *\n978\t   *   memory             object — Node.js process memory snapshot via process.memoryUsage().\n979\t   *     rssBytes               number — resident set size in bytes.\n980\t   *     heapUsedBytes          number — active heap usage in bytes.\n981\t   *     heapTotalBytes         number — total heap allocated in bytes.\n982\t   *     externalBytes          number — external (C++/Buffer) memory in bytes.\n983\t   *\n984\t   *   failingFeedsCount  number — count of feeds where consecutiveFailures > 0 AND\n985\t   *                            lastSuccessAt !== null AND time since last success > 5 minutes.\n986\t   *\n987\t   * HTTP status code:\n988\t   *   200 — when fewer than UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD (3) feeds are failing\n989\t   *         (consecutiveFailures > 0, had prior success, last success > 5 min ago).\n990\t   *   503 — when 3 or more feeds meet the failing criteria above.\n991\t   *   Note: \"degraded\" status can coexist with a 200 response (e.g. one stale feed\n992\t   *         but fewer than 3 failing). 503 implies \"degraded\" but not vice versa.\n993\t   */\n994\t  app.get(\"/api/health\", (c) => {\n995\t    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n996\t    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n997\t    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n998\t    const feedStates = getFeedStates();\n999\t    const alertsStatus = getAlertsStatus();\n1000\t    const allFeedsOk = feedStates.every(\n1001\t      (f) => f.circuitOpenAt === null && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && !f.isStale\n1002\t    );\n1003\t    const alertsOk = !alertsStatus.circuitOpen && alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt !== null;\n1004\t\n1005\t    // Count feeds that have been failing for >5 minutes\n1006\t    const now = Date.now();\n1007\t    const failingFeeds = feedStates.filter(\n1008\t      (f) =>\n1009\t        f.consecutiveFailures > 0 && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && now - f.lastSuccessAt > 300_000\n1010\t    );\n1011\t    const unhealthy = failingFeeds.length >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD;\n1012\t\n1013\t    const status = allFeedsOk && alertsOk ? \"ok\" : \"degraded\";\n1014\t    const httpStatus = unhealthy ? 503 : 200;\n1015\t\n1016\t    const memUsage = process.memoryUsage();\n1017\t    const allMetrics = metrics.getAll();\n1018\t\n1019\t    // Get cache hit rate from metrics (sum across all label combinations)\n1020\t    let cacheHitsValue = 0;\n1021\t    let cacheMissesValue = 0;\n1022\t    const cacheHitsMap = allMetrics.get(\"cache_hits_total\");\n1023\t    const cacheMissesMap = stale-while-revalidate=${CACHE_TTLS.gtfsStaticStale}`;\n\n/**\n * Common abbreviation mappings for station search\n */\nconst ABBREVIATIONS: Record<string, string> = {\n  sq: \"square\",\n  st: \"street\",\n  ave: \"avenue\",\n  av: \"avenue\",\n  blvd: \"boulevard\",\n  pkwy: \"parkway\",\n  rd: \"road\",\n  dr: \"drive\",\n  ln: \"lane\",\n  ct: \"court\",\n  pl: \"place\",\n  hwy: \"highway\",\n  expwy: \"expressway\",\n  bway: \"broadway\",\n  \"'way\": \"way\",\n};\n\nfunction expandAbbreviations(term: string): string {\n  const lower = term.toLowerCase();\n  if (ABBREVIATIONS[lower]) {\n    return ABBREVIATIONS[lower];\n  }\n  let expanded = term;\n  for (const [abbr, full] of Object.entries(ABBREVIATIONS)) {\n    const regex = new RegExp(`\\\\b${abbr}\\\\b`, \"gi\");\n    expanded = expanded.replace(regex, full);\n  }\n  return expanded;\n}\n\nfunction normalizeForSearch(str: string): string {\n  return str\n    .toLowerCase()\n    .replace(/[^a-z0-9\\s]/g, \"\")\n    .replace(/\\s+/g, \" \")\n    .trim();\n}\n\nfunction stationMatchesQuery(\n  station: Station,\n  normalizedQuery: string,\n  originalQuery: string\n): boolean {\n  const normalizedName = normalizeForSearch(station.name);\n  const expandedQuery = normalizeForSearch(expandAbbreviations(originalQuery));\n  const expandedNormalizedQuery = normalizeForSearch(expandAbbreviations(normalizedQuery));\n\n  if (\n    normalizedName.includes(normalizedQuery) ||\n    normalizedName.includes(expandedQuery) ||\n    normalizedName.includes(expandedNormalizedQuery)\n  ) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  const upperQuery = originalQuery.toUpperCase();\n  if (station.lines.some((line) => line === upperQuery)) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  const expandedName = normalizeForSearch(expandAbbreviations(station.name));\n  if (expandedName.includes(expandedQuery) || expandedName.includes(expandedNormalizedQuery)) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  return false;\n}\n\nfunction scoreSearchResult(station: Station, query: string): number {\n  const normalizedQuery = normalizeForSearch(query);\n  const normalizedName = normalizeForSearch(station.name);\n  const upperQuery = query.toUpperCase();\n\n  if (station.lines.some((line) => line === upperQuery)) {\n    return 1000;\n  }\n\n  if (normalizedName.startsWith(normalizedQuery)) {\n    return 100;\n  }\n\n  const words = normalizedName.split(/\\s+/);\n  if (words.some((word) => word.startsWith(normalizedQuery))) {\n    return 50;\n  }\n\n  if (normalizedName.includes(normalizedQuery)) {\n    return 10;\n  }\n\n  return 1;\n}\n\nfunction buildStationToComplexMap(complexes: ComplexIndex): Map<string, StationComplex> {\n  const map = new Map<string, StationComplex>();\n  for (const complex of Object.values(complexes)) {\n    for (const stationId of complex.stations) {\n      map.set(stationId, complex);\n    }\n  }\n  return map;\n}\n\n/**\n * @param stations         Pre-loaded GTFS static station index\n * @param routes           Pre-loaded GTFS static route index\n * @param complexes        Pre-loaded station complexes (hashed filenames) */\nconst IMMUTABLE_CACHE_HEADER = \"public, max-age=31536000, immutable\";\n\n/**\n * Compression middleware for API responses.\n * Supports brotli, gzip, and deflate based on Accept-Encoding header.\n * Brotli is preferred for best compression ratio.\n */\nfunction compressionMiddleware(): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    await next();\n\n    const body = c.res.body;\n    if (!body || c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Encoding\")) return;\n\n    const contentType = c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Type\");\n    if (!contentType?.includes(\"json\") && !contentType?.includes(\"text\")) return;\n\n    const acceptEncoding = c.req.header(\"Accept-Encoding\") || \"\";\n    const contentLength = c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Length\");\n\n    // Skip small responses (not worth compressing)\n    if (contentLength && parseInt(contentLength, 10) < 500) return;\n\n    // Clone response to get the body as buffer\n    const buffer = Buffer.from(await c.res.clone().arrayBuffer());\n\n    // Prefer brotli for best compression, then gzip, then deflate\n    if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"br\")) {\n      const brotlied = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n        const brotli = createBrotliCompress();\n        brotli.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n        brotli.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n        brotli.on(\"error\", reject);\n        brotli.end(buffer);\n      });\n\n      c.res = new Response(brotlied, {\n        status: c.res.status,\n        headers: c.res.headers,\n      });\n      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"br\");\n      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n    } else if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"gzip\")) {\n      const gzipped = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n        const gzip = createGzip();\n        gzip.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n        gzip.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n        gzip.on(\"error\", reject);\n        gzip.end(buffer);\n      });\n\n      c.res = new Response(gzipped, {\n        status: c.res.status,\n        headers: c.res.headers,\n      });\n      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"gzip\");\n      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n    } else if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"deflate\")) {\n      const deflated = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n        const deflate = createDeflate();\n        deflate.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n        deflate.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n        deflate.on(\"error\", reject);\n        deflate.end(buffer);\n      });\n\n      c.res = new Response(deflated, {\n        status: c.res.status,\n        headers: c.res.headers,\n      });\n      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"deflate\");\n      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n    }\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a path has a content hash (for immutable caching).\n * Vite produces files like: assets/index-a1b2c3d4.js\n */\nfunction isHashedAsset(path: string): boolean {\n  // Match pattern: /assets/name-[hash].ext where hash is 8+ hex chars\n  return /\\/assets\\/[^/]+-[a-f0-9]{8,}\\.(js|css|svg|png|jpg|ico|woff2?)$/i.test(path);\n}\n\nexport function createApp(\n  stations: StationIndex,\n  routes: RouteIndex,\n  complexes: ComplexIndex,\n  transfers: Record<string, TransferConnection[]>,\n  webDistPath: string\n): Hono {\n  const app = new Hono();\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Lightweight readiness endpoint (before middleware for fast response)\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // GET /health — returns 200 with { status: \"ok\" } when the server is ready\n  // to handle requests.  Used by orchestrators (Playwright, Kubernetes, etc.)\n  // to detect when the HTTP server is listening and the database is reachable.\n  //\n  // Registered BEFORE all middleware so it responds within ~1ms regardless of\n  // rate-limit state, CSRF tokens, etc.  Only checks database connectivity\n  // (a single SELECT 1) — feed poller readiness is reported by /api/health.\n  app.get(\"/health\", () => {\n    try {\n      const db = getPushDatabase();\n      db.prepare(\"SELECT 1\").get();\n    } catch {\n      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ status: \"error\", message: \"database unreachable\" }), {\n        status: 503,\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"Cache-Control\": \"no-store\" },\n      });\n    }\n\n    return new Response(\n      JSON.stringify({\n        status: \"ok\",\n        uptime_seconds: Math.floor((Date.now() - SERVER_START_MS) / 1000),\n      }),\n      {\n        status: 200,\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"Cache-Control\": \"no-store\" },\n      }\n    );\n  });\n\n  // Request ID for correlation across logs and audit events.\n  // Must run before security logging and audit middleware.\n  app.use(\"*\", requestId);\n\n  // Security headers on all responses (CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy)\n  // CSP includes report-uri for violation monitoring at /api/security/csp-report\n  app.use(\n    \"*\",\n    securityHeaders({\n      reportUri: \"/api/security/csp-report\",\n    })\n  );\n\n  // Security logging for all requests (OWASP A09: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures)\n  // Logs authentication failures, authorization failures, rate limit exceeded, and blocked attacks\n  app.use(\"*\", securityLogging());\n\n  // HTTP method restrictions (OWASP A01, A05)\n  // Blocks dangerous HTTP methods (TRACE, CONNECT) that could be used for attacks\n  app.use(\"*\", httpMethodRestrictions());\n\n  // HTTP Request Smuggling protection (OWASP A01, A03)\n  // Detects and blocks request smuggling attempts\n  app.use(\"*\", httpRequestSmuggling());\n\n  // HTTP Response Splitting protection (OWASP A01, A03)\n  // Detects and blocks CRLF injection attempts\n  app.use(\"*\", httpResponseSplitting());\n\n  // Host header protection to prevent cache poisoning and password reset poisoning\n  // In production, set ALLOWED_HOSTS environment variable to restrict allowed hosts\n  const isProduction = process.env[\"NODE_ENV\"] === \"production\";\n  const allowedHosts = process.env[\"ALLOWED_HOSTS\"]\n    ? process.env[\"ALLOWED_HOSTS\"].split(\",\")\n    : undefined;\n  app.use(\n    \"*\",\n    hostHeaderProtection({\n      allowedHosts,\n      blockMissingHost: isProduction, // Only require Host header in production\n      blockIpAddresses: isProduction,\n      blockPrivateNetworks: isProduction,\n      blockLocalhost: isProduction, // Block localhost requests in production\n    })\n  );\n\n  // Distributed tracing for all requests\n  app.use(\"*\", tracingMiddleware);\n\n  // Request size limits for all routes (DoS protection)\n  app.use(\"*\", requestSizeLimits());\n\n  // Input sanitization for all API routes (XSS, SQL injection prevention)\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", inputSanitization());\n\n  // JSON depth protection for API routes (OWASP A01, A03)\n  // Prevents DoS via deeply nested JSON structures\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", jsonDepthProtection());\n\n  // Optional authentication for all API routes\n  // Parses Authorization header and sets auth context if present\n  // Individual routes can require specific permissions using requirePermission\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", optionalAuth({ allowSessions: true }));\n\n  // CSRF protection for state-changing operations\n  // Excludes health, metrics, and safe read-only endpoints\n  // NOTE: /status is a public read-only HTML page (not under /api/*, so not affected)\n  // NOTE: /api/context, /api/auth/oauth, /api/auth/mfa, /api/auth/session are disabled\n  // NOTE: /api/auth/password is enabled for password reset functionality\n  app.use(\n    \"/api/*\",\n    csrfProtection({\n      excludePaths: [\n        \"/api/health\",\n        \"/api/metrics\",\n        \"/api/stations\",\n        \"/api/routes\",\n        \"/api/static\",\n        \"/api/arrivals\",\n        \"/api/alerts\",\n        \"/api/equipment\",\n        \"/api/trip\",\n        \"/api/positions\",\n        \"/api/push/vapid-public-key\",\n        \"/api/journal\",\n        // \"/api/context\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n        // \"/api/auth/oauth\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n        // \"/api/auth/mfa\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n        // \"/api/auth/session\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n        \"/api/auth/password\",\n        \"/api/csrf-token\",\n        \"/api/security/csp-report\", // CSP violation reports (browser-initiated)\n      ],\n    })\n  );\n\n  // HPP protection for all API routes (prevents parameter pollution attacks)\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", hppProtection({ strategy: \"first\" }));\n\n  // HTTP metrics collection for all API routes\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", httpMetrics());\n\n  // Rate limiting on all API routes (60 req/min per IP, token bucket)\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", rateLimiter());\n\n  // Response size limits for API routes (OWASP A01, A04)\n  // Prevents DoS via large response payloads\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", responseSizeLimits());\n\n  // Compression for API responses\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", compressionMiddleware());\n\n  const stationToComplex = buildStationToComplexMap(complexes);\n\n  // Create transfer engine for commute analysis\n  const transferEngine = createTransferEngine({\n    stations,\n    routes,\n    transfers,\n    complexes,\n    getArrivals: (stationId: string) => {\n      return getArrivals(stationId);\n    },\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // CSRF token endpoint\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\n    \"/api/csrf-token\",\n    cors({\n      allowedOrigins: [\"*\"], // Allow all origins for CSRF token\n      allowedMethods: [\"GET\"],\n      allowedHeaders: [\"Content-Type\", \"Authorization\"],\n    }),\n    (c) => {\n      const token = generateCsrfToken();\n\n      return c.json({ token });\n    }\n  );\n\n  /**\n   * Format a timestamp as \"X ago\" for human-readable display\n   */\n  function timeAgo(isoString: string | null): string {\n    if (!isoString) return \"Never\";\n    const ms = Date.now() - new Date(isoString).getTime();\n    const seconds = Math.floor(ms / 1000);\n    if (seconds < 60) return `${seconds}s ago`;\n    const minutes = Math.floor(seconds / 60);\n    if (minutes < 60) return `${minutes}m ago`;\n    const hours = Math.floor(minutes / 60);\n    if (hours < 24) return `${hours}h ago`;\n    const days = Math.floor(hours / 24);\n    return `${days}d ago`;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Format bytes as human-readable (e.g., \"45.2 MB\")\n   */\n  function formatBytes(bytes: number): string {\n    const units = [\"B\", \"KB\", \"MB\", \"GB\"];\n    let i = 0;\n    while (bytes >= 1024 && i < units.length - 1) {\n      bytes /= 1024;\n      i++;\n    }\n    return `${bytes.toFixed(1)} ${units[i]}`;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Build the HTML response for the /status dashboard\n   */\n  function buildStatusHtml(healthData: {\n    status: string;\n    timestamp: string;\n    uptime_seconds: number;\n    feeds: Array<{\n      id: string;\n      name: string;\n      status: string;\n      lastSuccessAt: string | null;\n      lastPollAt: string | null;\n      consecutiveFailures: number;\n      entityCount: number;\n      lastError: string | null;\n      avgLatencyMs: number;\n      errorCount24h: number;\n    }>;\n    alerts: {\n      count: number;\n      lastSuccessAt: string | null;\n      matchRate: number;\n      consecutiveFailures: number;\n      circuitOpen: boolean;\n      unmatchedCount: number;\n    };\n    delayDetector: {\n      enabled: boolean;\n      activePredictions: number;\n      lastRunAt: string | null;\n    };\n    delayPredictor: {\n      enabled: boolean;\n      modelAccuracy: number;\n      lastTrainedAt: string | null;\n    };\n    equipment: {\n      lastSuccessAt: string | null;\n      outages: number;\n      consecutiveFailures: number;\n    };\n    pushSubscriptions: number;\n    cacheHitRate: number;\n    memory: {\n      rssBytes: number;\n      heapUsedBytes: number;\n      heapTotalBytes: number;\n      externalBytes: number;\n    };\n    failingFeedsCount: number;\n  }): string {\n    const uptimeHours = Math.floor(healthData.uptime_seconds / 3600);\n    const uptimeMinutes = Math.floor((healthData.uptime_seconds % 3600) / 60);\n    const statusColor =\n      healthData.status === \"ok\"\n        ? \"#22c55e\"\n        : healthData.failingFeedsCount >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD\n          ? \"#ef4444\"\n          : \"#f59e0b\";\n    const statusIcon =\n      healthData.status === \"ok\"\n        ? \"✓\"\n        : healthData.failingFeedsCount >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD\n          ? \"✗\"\n          : \"⚠\";\n\n    return `<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"en\">\n<head>\n  <meta charset=\"UTF-8\">\n  <meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0\">\n  <title>MTA My Way - System Status</title>\n  <style>\n    * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }\n    body {\n      font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, \"Segoe UI\", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;\n      background: #0f172a;\n      color: #e2e8f0;\n      line-height: 1.5;\n      padding: 20px;\n    }\n    .container { max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto; }\n    h1 { font-size: 24px; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 8px; }\n    .subtitle { color: #94a3b8; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 24px; }\n    .status-banner {\n      display: inline-flex;\n      align-items: center;\n      gap: 8px;\n      padding: 12px 20px;\n      border-radius: 8px;\n      font-weight: 600;\n      font-size: 16px;\n      margin-bottom: 24px;\n      background: ${statusColor}20;\n      border: 1px solid ${statusColor}40;\n    }\n    .status-icon { font-size: 20px; }\n    .status-text { color: ${statusColor}; }\n    .grid { display: grid; gap: 20px; }\n    .card {\n      background: #1e293b;\n      border-radius: 12px;\n      padding: 20px;\n      border: 1px solid #334155;\n    }\n    .card-title {\n      font-size: 16px;\n      font-weight: 600;\n      margin-bottom: 16px;\n      color: #f1f5f9;\n    }\n    .metric-grid {\n      display: grid;\n      grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(200px, 1fr));\n      gap: 16px;\n    }\n    .metric { background: #0f172a; padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; }\n    .metric-label { font-size: 12px; color: #94a3b8; margin-bottom: 4px; }\n    .metric-value { font-size: 20px; font-weight: 600; color: #f1f5f9; }\n    .metric-sub { font-size: 12px; color: #64748b; margin-top: 4px; }\n    .feed-table {\n      width: 100%;\n      border-collapse: collapse;\n      font-size: 14px;\n    }\n    .feed-table th {\n      text-align: left;\n      padding: 12px;\n      border-bottom: 1px solid #334155;\n      color: #94a3b8;\n      font-weight: 500;\n    }\n    .feed-table td {\n      padding: 12px;\n      border-bottom: 1px solid #1e293b;\n    }\n    .feed-table tr:last-child td { border-bottom: none; }\n    .status-badge {\n      display: inline-block;\n      padding: 4px 10px;\n      border-radius: 12px;\n      font-size: 12px;\n      font-weight: 500;\n    }\n    .status-ok { background: #22c55e20; color: #22c55e; }\n    .status-stale { background: #f59e0b20; color: #f59e0b; }\n    .status-circuit_open { background: #ef444420; color: #ef4444; }\n    .status-never_polled { background: #64748b20; color: #94a3b8; }\n    .last-updated {\n      text-align: center;\n      color: #64748b;\n      font-size: 12px;\n      margin-top: 32px;\n      padding-top: 16px;\n      border-top: 1px solid #1e293b;\n    }\n    @media (max-width: 640px) {\n      .feed-table { display: block; overflow-x: auto; }\n      .metric-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }\n    }\n  </style>\n</head>\n<body>\n  <div class=\"container\">\n    <h1>MTA My Way</h1>\n    <p class=\"subtitle\">Real-time subway data system status</p>\n\n    <div class=\"status-banner\">\n      <span class=\"status-icon\">${statusIcon}</span>\n      <span class=\"status-text\">${healthData.status.toUpperCase()}</span>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"grid\">\n      <!-- System Overview -->\n      <div class=\"card\">\n        <div class=\"card-title\">System Overview</div>\n        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Uptime</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${uptimeHours}h ${uptimeMinutes}m</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Cache Hit Rate</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${(healthData.cacheHitRate * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Push Subscriptions</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${healthData.pushSubscriptions}</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Failing Feeds</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\" style=\"color: ${healthData.failingFeedsCount > 0 ? \"#ef4444\" : \"#22c55e\"}\">${healthData.failingFeedsCount}</div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n\n      <!-- Memory Usage -->\n      <div class=\"card\">\n        <div class=\"card-title\">Memory Usage</div>\n        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">RSS (Total)</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${formatBytes(healthData.memory.rssBytes)}</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Heap Used</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${formatBytes(healthData.memory.heapUsedBytes)}</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-sub\">of ${formatBytes(healthData.memory.heapTotalBytes)}</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">External</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${formatBytes(healthData.memory.externalBytes)}</div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n\n      <!-- GTFS-RT Feeds -->\n      <div class=\"card\">\n        <div class=\"card-title\">MTA Feeds (GTFS-RT)</div>\n        <table class=\"feed-table\">\n          <thead>\n            <tr>\n              <th>Feed</th>\n              <th>Status</th>\n              <th>Last Success</th>\n              <th>Entities</th>\n              <th>Latency</th>\n              <th>Errors (24h)</th>\n            </tr>\n          </thead>\n          <tbody>\n            ${healthData.feeds\n              .map(\n                (feed) => `\n            <tr>\n              <td>${feed.name}</td>\n              <td><span class=\"status-badge status-${feed.status}\">${feed.status.replace(/_/g, \" \")}</span></td>\n              <td>${timeAgo(feed.lastSuccessAt)}</td>\n              <td>${feed.entityCount}</td>\n              <td>${feed.avgLatencyMs}ms</td>\n              <td>${feed.errorCount24h}</td>\n            </tr>\n          `\n              )\n              .join(\"\")}\n          </tbody>\n        </table>\n      </div>\n\n      <!-- Alerts Status -->\n      <div class=\"card\">\n        <div class=\"card-title\">Service Alerts</div>\n        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Active Alerts</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${healthData.alerts.count}</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Match Rate</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${(healthData.alerts.matchRate * 100).toFixed(0)}%</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-sub\">${healthData.alerts.unmatchedCount} unmatched</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Last Update</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${timeAgo(healthData.alerts.lastSuccessAt)}</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Status</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">\n              <span class=\"status-badge status-${healthData.alerts.circuitOpen ? \"circuit_open\" : \"ok\"}\">\n                ${healthData.alerts.circuitOpen ? \"Circuit Open\" : \"OK\"}\n              </span>\n            </div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n\n      <!-- Delay Detection -->\n      <div class=\"card\">\n        <div class=\"card-title\">Delay Detection</div>\n        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Detector</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">\n              <span class=\"status-badge status-${healthData.delayDetector.enabled ? \"ok\" : \"never_polled\"}\">\n                ${healthData.delayDetector.enabled ? \"Enabled\" : \"Disabled\"}\n              </span>\n            </div>\n            <div class=\"metric-sub\">${healthData.delayDetector.activePredictions} active predictions</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Predictor</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">\n              <span class=\"status-badge status-${healthData.delayPredictor.enabled ? \"ok\" : \"never_polled\"}\">\n                ${healthData.delayPredictor.enabled ? \"Enabled\" : \"Disabled\"}\n              </span>\n            </div>\n            <div class=\"metric-sub\">${(healthData.delayPredictor.modelAccuracy * 100).toFixed(0)}% accuracy</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Equipment</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${healthData.equipment.outages}</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-sub\">active outages</div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"last-updated\">\n      Last updated: ${new Date(healthData.timestamp).toLocaleString()} •\n      <a href=\"/api/health\" style=\"color: #38bdf8;\">View JSON</a>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n</body>\n</html>`;\n  }\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Health endpoint\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  /**\n   * GET /api/health — Per-feed health status (JSON)\n   *\n   * Query validation: Uses `emptyQuerySchema` (`z.object({}).strict()`).\n   * Any query parameters at all cause a 400 with `{ error: \"validation failed\", details: [...] }`.\n   * No query parameters are accepted.\n   *\n   * Response top-level fields:\n   *   status              (\"ok\" | \"degraded\")\n   *     — \"ok\" when all feeds have circuitOpenAt===null AND lastSuccessAt!==null AND !isStale,\n   *       AND alerts has circuitOpen===false AND lastSuccessAt!==null.\n   *     — \"degraded\" otherwise.\n   *\n   *   timestamp           string (ISO 8601) — new Date().toISOString() at request time.\n   *\n   *   uptime_seconds      number — seconds since SERVER_START_MS (set at module load).\n   *\n   *   feeds               Array<FeedState> — one entry per GTFS-realtime feed.\n   *     Each element:\n   *       id                    string   — feed identifier (e.g. \"1\", \"26\", \"31\", \"36\").\n   *       name                  string   — human-readable feed name.\n   *       status                (\"circuit_open\" | \"never_polled\" | \"stale\" | \"ok\")\n   *         — \"circuit_open\" when circuitOpenAt !== null.\n   *         — \"never_polled\" when lastSuccessAt === null.\n   *         — \"stale\" when isStale === true.\n   *         — \"ok\" otherwise.\n   *       lastSuccessAt         string | null — ISO 8601 of last successful poll, or null.\n   *       lastPollAt            string | null — ISO 8601 of last poll attempt, or null.\n   *       consecutiveFailures  number — count of consecutive failed polls.\n   *       entityCount           number — number of entities in the feed.\n   *       lastError             string | null — truncated (max 100 chars) last error message,\n   *                                  newlines stripped; null if no error.\n   *       tripReplacementPeriod string | null — the feed's trip replacement period.\n   *       avgLatencyMs          number — average of latencyHistory entries.\n   *       errorCount24h         number — count of error timestamps within the last 24 hours.\n   *       parseErrors           (feed-specific type) — parse error details for the feed.\n   *\n   *   alerts              object — alerts polling subsystem status.\n   *     count                  number  — number of alerts tracked.\n   *     lastSuccessAt          number | null — epoch-ms of last successful alert fetch, or null.\n   *     matchRate              number  — fraction of alerts that matched trips (0–1).\n   *     consecutiveFailures    number  — consecutive alert fetch failures.\n   *     circuitOpen            boolean — whether the alerts circuit breaker is open.\n   *     unmatchedCount         number  — alerts that did not match any known trip.\n   *\n   *   delayDetector       object — delay detector subsystem status.\n   *     trackedTrips           number — count of trips currently being tracked.\n   *     activeAlerts           number — count of active predicted delay alerts.\n   *     thresholdMultiplier    number — delay threshold configuration multiplier.\n   *     minTrainsForLineAlert  number — minimum trains needed to trigger a line-level alert.\n   *\n   *   delayPredictor     object — delay predictor subsystem status.\n   *     totalRecords           number — count of delay records stored.\n   *     aggregatedPatterns     number — count of aggregated statistical patterns.\n   *     minObservations        number — minimum observations required for a prediction.\n   *     currentWeather         WeatherCondition — current weather string (e.g. \"clear\").\n   *\n   *   equipment           object (EquipmentCacheStatus) — elevator/escalator outage status.\n   *     lastFetchAt            string | null — ISO 8601 of last fetch attempt.\n   *     lastSuccessAt          string | null — ISO 8601 of last successful fetch.\n   *     outageCount            number — number of stations with tracked outages.\n   *     consecutiveFailures    number — consecutive equipment fetch failures.\n   *     circuitOpen            boolean — whether the equipment circuit breaker is open.\n   *\n   *   pushSubscriptions   number — count of active push notification subscriptions.\n   *\n   *   cacheHitRate        number — fraction 0–1, computed as cache_hits_total /\n   *                            (cache_hits_total + cache_misses_total) summed across all\n   *                            label combinations. Rounded to 2 decimal places.\n   *                            Returns 0 when there have been no cache requests.\n   *\n   *   memory             object — Node.js process memory snapshot via process.memoryUsage().\n   *     rssBytes               number — resident set size in bytes.\n   *     heapUsedBytes          number — active heap usage in bytes.\n   *     heapTotalBytes         number — total heap allocated in bytes.\n   *     externalBytes          number — external (C++/Buffer) memory in bytes.\n   *\n   *   failingFeedsCount  number — count of feeds where consecutiveFailures > 0 AND\n   *                            lastSuccessAt !== null AND time since last success > 5 minutes.\n   *\n   * HTTP status code:\n   *   200 — when fewer than UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD (3) feeds are failing\n   *         (consecutiveFailures > 0, had prior success, last success > 5 min ago).\n   *   503 — when 3 or more feeds meet the failing criteria above.\n   *   Note: \"degraded\" status can coexist with a 200 response (e.g. one stale feed\n   *         but fewer than 3 failing). 503 implies \"degraded\" but not vice versa.\n   */\n  app.get(\"/api/health\", (c) => {\n    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n    const feedStates = getFeedStates();\n    const alertsStatus = getAlertsStatus();\n    const allFeedsOk = feedStates.every(\n      (f) => f.circuitOpenAt === null && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && !f.isStale\n    );\n    const alertsOk = !alertsStatus.circuitOpen && alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt !== null;\n\n    // Count feeds that have been failing for >5 minutes\n    const now = Date.now();\n    const failingFeeds = feedStates.filter(\n      (f) =>\n        f.consecutiveFailures > 0 && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && now - f.lastSuccessAt > 300_000\n    );\n    const unhealthy = failingFeeds.length >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD;\n\n    const status = allFeedsOk && alertsOk ? \"ok\" : \"degraded\";\n    const httpStatus = unhealthy ? 503 : 200;\n\n    const memUsage = process.memoryUsage();\n    const allMetrics = metrics.getAll();\n\n    // Get cache hit rate from metrics (sum across all label combinations)\n    let cacheHitsValue = 0;\n    let cacheMissesValue = 0;\n    const cacheHitsMap = allMetrics.get(\"cache_hits_total\");\n    const cacheMissesMap = allMetrics.get(\"cache_misses_total\");\n    if (cacheHitsMap) {\n      for (const labeled of cacheHitsMap.values()) {\n        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n          cacheHitsValue += labeled.metric.value;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    if (cacheMissesMap) {\n      for (const labeled of cacheMissesMap.values()) {\n        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n          cacheMissesValue += labeled.metric.value;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    const totalCacheRequests = cacheHitsValue + cacheMissesValue;\n    const cacheHitRate =\n      totalCacheRequests > 0 ? Math.round((cacheHitsValue / totalCacheRequests) * 100) / 100 : 0;\n\n    return c.json(\n      {\n        status,\n        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n        uptime_seconds: Math.floor((Date.now() - SERVER_START_MS) / 1000),\n        feeds: feedStates.map((f) => ({\n          id: f.id,\n          name: f.name,\n          status:\n            f.circuitOpenAt !== null\n              ? \"circuit_open\"\n              : f.lastSuccessAt === null\n                ? \"never_polled\"\n                : f.isStale\n                  ? \"stale\"\n                  : \"ok\",\n          lastSuccessAt: f.lastSuccessAt ? new Date(f.lastSuccessAt).toISOString() : null,\n          lastPollAt: f.lastPollAt ? new Date(f.lastPollAt).toISOString() : null,\n          consecutiveFailures: f.consecutiveFailures,\n          entityCount: f.entityCount,\n          lastError: f.lastErrorMessage\n            ? f.lastErrorMessage.slice(0, 100).replace(/[\\r\\n]/g, \" \")\n            : null,\n          tripReplacementPeriod: f.tripReplacementPeriod,\n          avgLatencyMs: avgLatency(f.latencyHistory),\n          errorCount24h: errorCount24h(f.errorTimestamps),\n          parseErrors: f.parseErrors,\n        })),\n        alerts: {\n          count: alertsStatus.alertCount,\n          lastSuccessAt: alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt,\n          matchRate: alertsStatus.matchRate,\n          consecutiveFailures: alertsStatus.consecutiveFailures,\n          circuitOpen: alertsStatus.circuitOpen,\n          unmatchedCount: alertsStatus.unmatchedCount,\n        },\n        delayDetector: getDelayDetectorStatus(),\n        delayPredictor: getDelayPredictorStatus(),\n        equipment: getEquipmentStatus(),\n        pushSubscriptions: getSubscriptionCount(),\n        cacheHitRate,\n        memory: {\n          rssBytes: memUsage.rss,\n          heapUsedBytes: memUsage.heapUsed,\n          heapTotalBytes: memUsage.heapTotal,\n          externalBytes: memUsage.external,\n        },\n        failingFeedsCount: failingFeeds.length,\n      },\n      httpStatus as 200 | 503\n    );\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Public status page (human-readable dashboard)\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/status\", (c) => {\n    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    // Fetch the same data as the health endpoint\n    const feedStates = getFeedStates();\n    const alertsStatus = getAlertsStatus();\n    const allFeedsOk = feedStates.every(\n      (f) => f.circuitOpenAt === null && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && !f.isStale\n    );\n    const alertsOk = !alertsStatus.circuitOpen && alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt !== null;\n\n    // Count feeds that have been failing for >5 minutes\n    const now = Date.now();\n    const failingFeeds = feedStates.filter(\n      (f) =>\n        f.consecutiveFailures > 0 && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && now - f.lastSuccessAt > 300_000\n    );\n    const unhealthy = failingFeeds.length >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD;\n\n    const status = allFeedsOk && alertsOk ? \"ok\" : \"degraded\";\n    const httpStatus = unhealthy ? 503 : 200;\n\n    const memUsage = process.memoryUsage();\n    const allMetrics = metrics.getAll();\n\n    // Get cache hit rate from metrics\n    let cacheHitsValue = 0;\n    let cacheMissesValue = 0;\n    const cacheHitsMap = allMetrics.get(\"cache_hits_total\");\n    const cacheMissesMap = allMetrics.get(\"cache_misses_total\");\n    if (cacheHitsMap) {\n      for (const labeled of cacheHitsMap.values()) {\n        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n          cacheHitsValue += labeled.metric.value;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    if (cacheMissesMap) {\n      for (const labeled of cacheMissesMap.values()) {\n        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n          cacheMissesValue += labeled.metric.value;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    const totalCacheRequests = cacheHitsValue + cacheMissesValue;\n    const cacheHitRate =\n      totalCacheRequests > 0 ? Math.round((cacheHitsValue / totalCacheRequests) * 100) / 100 : 0;\n\n    const healthData = {\n      status,\n      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n      uptime_seconds: Math.floor((Date.now() - SERVER_START_MS) / 1000),\n      feeds: feedStates.map((f) => ({\n        id: f.id,\n        name: f.name,\n        status:\n          f.circuitOpenAt !== null\n            ? \"circuit_open\"\n            : f.lastSuccessAt === null\n              ? \"never_polled\"\n              : f.isStale\n                ? \"stale\"\n                : \"ok\",\n        lastSuccessAt: f.lastSuccessAt ? new Date(f.lastSuccessAt).toISOString() : null,\n        lastPollAt: f.lastPollAt ? new Date(f.lastPollAt).toISOString() : null,\n        consecutiveFailures: f.consecutiveFailures,\n        entityCount: f.entityCount,\n        lastError: f.lastErrorMessage\n          ? f.lastErrorMessage.slice(0, 100).replace(/[\\r\\n]/g, \" \")\n          : null,\n        avgLatencyMs: avgLatency(f.latencyHistory),\n        errorCount24h: errorCount24h(f.errorTimestamps),\n      })),\n      alerts: {\n        count: alertsStatus.alertCount,\n        lastSuccessAt: alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt,\n        matchRate: alertsStatus.matchRate,\n        consecutiveFailures: alertsStatus.consecutiveFailures,\n        circuitOpen: alertsStatus.circuitOpen,\n        unmatchedCount: alertsStatus.unmatchedCount,\n      },\n      delayDetector: getDelayDetectorStatus(),\n      delayPredictor: getDelayPredictorStatus(),\n      equipment: getEquipmentStatus(),\n      pushSubscriptions: getSubscriptionCount(),\n      cacheHitRate,\n      memory: {\n        rssBytes: memUsage.rss,\n        heapUsedBytes: memUsage.heapUsed,\n        heapTotalBytes: memUsage.heapTotal,\n        externalBytes: memUsage.external,\n      },\n      failingFeedsCount: failingFeeds.length,\n    };\n\n    const html = buildStatusHtml(healthData);\n    c.header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/html; charset=utf-8\");\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", \"public, max-age=30\");\n    return c.html(html, httpStatus as 200 | 503);\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Metrics export endpoint (for Prometheus)\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/api/metrics\", (c) => {\n    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    const metricsText = metrics.exportPrometheus();\n    c.header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain; version=0.0.4; charset=utf-8\");\n    return c.text(metricsText);\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // CSP violation reporting endpoint\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.post(\"/api/security/csp-report\", async (c) => {\n    try {\n      const contentType = c.req.header(\"Content-Type\") ?? \"\";\n      const baseContentType = contentType.split(\";\")[0]!.trim().toLowerCase();\n      const allowed = [\"application/csp-report\", \"application/json\", \"application/reports+json\"];\n      if (!allowed.includes(baseContentType)) {\n        return c.json({ error: \"Unsupported Media Type\" }, 415);\n      }\n\n      const report = await c.req.json().catch(() => null);\n\n      if (!report) {\n        return c.json({ error: \"Invalid report\" }, 400);\n      }\n\n      // Log the CSP violation for security monitoring\n      logger.warn(\"CSP violation detected\", {\n        \"user-agent\": c.req.header(\"user-agent\"),\n        referrer: c.req.header(\"referrer\"),\n        \"x-forwarded-for\": c.req.header(\"x-forwarded-for\")?.split(\",\")[0] ?? \"unknown\",\n        report,\n      });\n\n      // Return 200 to acknowledge the report\n      return c.json({ received: true });\n    } catch (error) {\n      logger.error(\"Failed to process CSP report\", error as Error);\n      return c.json({ error: \"Failed to process report\" }, 400);\n    }\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Security disclosure policy (RFC 9116)\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/.well-known/security.txt\", (c) => {\n    const contact = process.env[\"SECURITY_CONTACT\"] ?? \"mailto:security@jedarden.com\";\n    const body =\n      `Contact: ${contact}\\n` +\n      `Preferred-Languages: en\\n` +\n      `Policy: https://github.com/jedarden/mta-my-way/security/policy\\n`;\n    c.header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain; charset=utf-8\");\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", \"max-age=86400\");\n    return c.text(body);\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Real-time arrivals\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/api/arrivals/:id\", (c) => {\n    const params = validateParams(c, stationIdParamsSchema);\n    if (params instanceof Response) return params;\n\n    const { id: stationId } = params;\n    const arrivals = getArrivals(stationId);\n\n    if (!arrivals) {\n      return c.json({ error: \"Station not found or no data yet\" }, 404);\n    }\n\n    // Inject equipment status into arrivals response\n    const equipmentSummary = getEquipmentForStation(stationId);\n    const response = {\n      ...arrivals,\n      equipment: equipmentSummary?.equipment ?? [],\n    };\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTLS.api}`);\n    return c.json(response);\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // GTFS static station data\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/api/stations\", (c) => {\n    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", STATIC_CACHE_HEADER);\n    return c.json(Object.values(stations));\n  });\n\n  app.get(\"/api/stations/search\", (c) => {\n    const startTime = Date.now();\n    const query = validateQuery(c, stationSearchQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    const { q } = query;\n    const trimmedQuery = q.trim();\n    const normalizedQuery = normalizeForSearch(trimmedQuery);\n\n    const results = Object.values(stations)\n      .filter((station) => stationMatchesQuery(station, normalizedQuery, trimmedQuery))\n      .map((station) => ({\n        station,\n        score: scoreSearchResult(station, trimmedQuery),\n      }))\n      .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)\n      .map(({ station }) => station);\n\n    const duration = (Date.now() - startTime) / 1000;\n    recordStationSearchDuration(duration);\n    recordStationSearchRequest(results.length);\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", STATIC_CACHE_HEADER);\n    return c.json(results);\n  });\n\n  app.get(\"/api/stations/:id\", (c) => {\n    const params = validateParams(c, stationIdParamsSchema);\n    if (params instanceof Response) return params;\n\n    const { id } = params;\n    const station = stations[id];\n\n    if (!station) {\n      return c.json({ error: \"Station not found\" }, 404);\n    }\n\n    const complex = stationToComplex.get(id);\n\n    const response: Record<string, unknown> = {\n      ...station,\n      complexStations: [] as Station[],\n      complexLines: station.lines,\n    };\n\n    if (complex) {\n      const complexStations = complex.stations\n        .map((sid) => stations[sid])\n        .filter((s): s is Station => s !== undefined);\n\n      const allLines = new Set<string>();\n      for (const s of complexStations) {\n        for (const line of s.lines) {\n          allLines.add(line);\n        }\n      }\n\n      response.complexId = complex.complexId;\n      response.complexName = complex.name;\n      response.complexStations = complexStations;\n      response.complexLines = Array.from(allLines).sort();\n    }\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", STATIC_CACHE_HEADER);\n    return c.json(response);\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // GTFS static route data\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/api/routes\", (c) => {\n    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", STATIC_CACHE_HEADER);\n    return c.json(Object.values(routes));\n  });\n\n  app.get(\"/api/routes/:id\", (c) => {\n    const params = validateParams(c, routeIdParamsSchema);\n    if (params instanceof Response) return params;\n\n    const { id } = params;\n    const route = routes[id];\n\n    if (!route) {\n      return c.json({ error: \"Route not found\" }, 404);\n    }\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", STATIC_CACHE_HEADER);\n    return c.json(route);\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Static GTFS data (complexes)\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/api/static/complexes\", (c) => {\n    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", STATIC_CACHE_HEADER);\n    return c.json(Object.values(complexes));\n  });\n\n  app.get(\"/api/static/complexes/:id\", (c) => {\n    const params = c.json({ error: \"Complex not found\" }, 404);\n    }\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", STATIC_CACHE_HEADER);\n    return c.json(complex);\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Commute analysis - requires authentication for personal commute tracking\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.post(\n    \"/api/commute/analyze\",\n    requireResourceAccess(\"commute\", \"create\"),\n    requirePermission(\"commutes:create\" as Permission),\n    auditLogAccess(\"commute\", \"create\"),\n    async (c) => {\n      const startTime = Date.now();\n      let success = false;\n      let hasTransfers = false;\n      const accessibleMode = false;\n\n      try {\n        const body = await validateBody(c, commuteAnalyzeRequestSchema);\n        if (body instanceof Response) return body;\n\n        const {\n          originId,\n          destinationId,\n          preferredLines = [],\n          commuteId = \"default\",\n          accessibleMode: accessible = false,\n        } = body;\n\n        const accessibleMode = accessible;\n\n        if (!stations[originId]) {\n          return c.json({ error: `Origin station not found: ${originId}` }, 404);\n        }\n        if (!stations[destinationId]) {\n          return c.json({ error: `Destination station not found: ${destinationId}` }, 404);\n        }\n\n        const analysis = transferEngine.analyzeCommute(\n          originId,\n          destinationId,\n          preferredLines,\n          commuteId,\n          accessibleMode\n        );\n\n        hasTransfers = analysis.transferRoutes.length > 0;\n        success = true;\n\n        const duration = (Date.now() - startTime) / 1000;\n        recordCommuteAnalysisDuration(duration);\n        recordCommuteAnalysisRequest(success, hasTransfers, accessibleMode);\n\n        c.header(\"Cache-Control\", `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTLS.api}`);\n        return c.json(analysis);\n      } catch (error) {\n        logger.error(\"Commute analysis error\", error instanceof Error ? error : undefined);\n\n        const duration = (Date.now() - startTime) / 1000;\n        recordCommuteAnalysisDuration(duration);\n        recordCommuteAnalysisRequest(false, false, accessibleMode);\n\n        return c.json({ error: \"Failed to analyze commute\" }, 500);\n      }\n    }\n  );\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Alerts\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/api/alerts\", (c) => {\n    // Validate query parameters to prevent injection attacks\n    const query = validateQuery(c, alertsQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    // Apply filtering if query parameters are provided\n    let officialAlerts = getAllAlerts();\n    if (query.lineId) {\n      officialAlerts = getAlertsForLine(query.lineId);\n    }\n    if (query.activeOnly) {\n      const now = Date.now() / 1000; // Convert to seconds for comparison\n      officialAlerts = officialAlerts.filter((a) => {\n        const start = a.activePeriod.start;\n        const end = a.activePeriod.end;\n        // Alert is active if we're past the start time and either no end or before end\n        return now >= start && (!end || now <= end);\n      });\n    }\n\n    const predictedAlerts = getPredictedAlerts();\n    const status = getAlertsStatus();\n    const delayDetector = getDelayDetectorStatus();\n\n    // Merge official + predicted alerts; predicted are tagged with source field\n    const alerts = [...officialAlerts, ...predictedAlerts];\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTLS.api}`);\n    return c.json({\n      alerts,\n      meta: {\n        count: alerts.length,\n        officialCount: officialAlerts.length,\n        predictedCount: predictedAlerts.length,\n        lastUpdatedAt: status.lastSuccessAt,\n        matchRate: status.matchRate,\n        consecutiveFailures: status.consecutiveFailures,\n        circuitOpen: status.circuitOpen,\n        delayDetector,\n      },\n    });\n  });\n\n  app.get(\"/api/alerts/:lineId\", (c) => {\n    const params = validateParams(c, lineIdParamsSchema);\n    if (params instanceof Response) return params;\n\n    const { lineId } = params;\n    const officialAlerts = getAlertsForLine(lineId);\n    const predictedAlerts = getPredictedAlerts().filter((a) => a.affectedLines.includes(lineId));\n    const alerts = [...officialAlerts, ...predictedAlerts];\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTLS.api}`);\n    return c.json({ alerts, lineId });\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Equipment (elevator/escalator outages)\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/api/equipment\", (c) => {\n    // Validate query parameters to prevent injection attacks\n    const query = validateQuery(c, equipmentQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    // Apply filtering if query parameters are provided\n    let summaries = getAllEquipment();\n    if (query.stationId) {\n      const summary = getEquipmentForStation(query.stationId);\n      return c.json(summary ? { stations: [summary], count: 1 } : { stations: [], count: 0 });\n    }\n    if (query.type && query.type !== \"all\") {\n      summaries = summaries.filter((s) =>\n        s.equipment.some((e) => e.type.toLowerCase() === query.type!.toLowerCase())\n      );\n    }\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", `public,   return c.json({ stationId, equipment: [], adaAccessible: true }, 200);\n    }\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTLS.api}`);\n    return c.json(summary);\n  });\n\n  //
